note: Pre-computed demo fallback — used when GLITCH_USE_DEMO_DOC=true

techniqueSteps.name: Grip
techniqueSteps.description: Pinch grip: thumb and index finger pinch the blade just above the bolster. Remaining three fingers wrap the handle. This gives control and reduces fatigue.

techniqueSteps.name: Claw hand
techniqueSteps.description: Curl fingertips under, knuckles forward. Knuckles guide the blade side — the knife never contacts fingertips.

techniqueSteps.name: Push cut
techniqueSteps.description: Lead with the heel, push forward and down through the ingredient. Wrist stays level. Blade stays in contact with the knuckle guide throughout the stroke.

techniqueSteps.name: Rock chop
techniqueSteps.description: Tip stays on board. Pivot from the tip, rocking the blade down and forward. Good for herbs and fine mince.

techniqueSteps.name: Julienne
techniqueSteps.description: Square the vegetable first (four flat sides). Slice into planks of equal thickness. Stack planks, cut into matchsticks. Consistent thickness = even cooking.

commonMistakes.observable: Fingertips extend past knuckles toward blade
commonMistakes.correction: Curl fingertips under — knuckles act as the blade guide

commonMistakes.mistake: Lifting the knife fully off the board between cuts
commonMistakes.observable: Blade rises more than 3cm between strokes
commonMistakes.correction: Keep blade tip in contact with board; rock or slide — don't lift

commonMistakes.mistake: Wrist higher than elbow during push cut
commonMistakes.observable: Wrist angle breaks upward; uneven slice thickness
commonMistakes.correction: Level wrist, drive motion from shoulder and elbow

commonMistakes.observable: Full palm on handle, no thumb/index contact with blade
commonMistakes.correction: Move hand forward into pinch grip — one inch toward the blade

progressionMilestones: Consistent claw hand with no fingertip exposure (session 1)
Pinch grip feels natural; no handle-only grip (session 1-2)
Uniform slice thickness within 2mm on carrots (session 2-3)
Push cut rhythm without pausing between strokes (session 3-4)
Clean julienne on a zucchini: 5mm x 5mm matchsticks (session 4-6)

keyTimestamps.topic: Push cut vs rock chop comparison
